subject: ap-chemistry
title: Solubility Equilibrium & Ksp
stimulus: Answer the following questions about the solubility of $Ca(OH)_2$ ($K_{sp}$ = 1.3 × 10$^{-6}$).

parts.prompt: Write a balanced chemical equation for the dissolution of $Ca(OH)_2$(s) in pure water.

scoring_points.description: Provide the correct identification or prediction

parts.prompt:
Calculate the molar solubility of $Ca(OH)_2$
in 0.10 M $Ca(NO_3)_2$.

scoring_points.description: Calculate the correct value with setup and answer

required_elements: Correct mathematical setup or formula application
Correct numerical answer with appropriate sig figs and units

parts.prompt:
In the box below, complete a particle representation diagram that includes four water molecules with proper
orientation around the Ca2+ ion.
Represent water molecules as .
−
Na C H N S O + OCl → products
2 37 34 2 3 9
blue colorless

scoring_points.description: Provide a correct response for part (c)
